text:
Once upon a time, in a quaint village nestled between rolling hills and a sparkling river, there lived a man named Oliver. Oliver was known among the villagers for his deep love of antique books and peculiar birds. He had a garden where he often sat, reading, and tended a bird feeder that attracted all sorts of interesting sparrows.

One peculiar morning, Oliver noticed a new kind of bird perched on his feeder. It had bright yellow feathers instead of the typical black ones. Oliver watched as it sang a song so unusual it could've been a tune from one of his favorite books. He named the bird Dulcis.

Dulcis became Oliver's faithful companion. They spent afternoons together wandering through the village, picking berries. One particular day, while chasing a butterfly, they stumbled upon an old windmill parked on the edge of the woods. A sign taped to the miller's door read "Quiet Hour 9am to 10am."

Curious, Oliver and Dulcis
